highlights.summary: Introduce a LoopGuard actor to serialize loop execution and prevent a race that could start the loop twice; move background-task handling into the loop() async scope (remove shared backgroundTaskID and previous expiration-Task); consolidate loop lifecycle functions into loop()/finalizeLoop(); make calculateLoopInterval accept loopStartDate; and reorder executeLoop to record end/duration/status and only call enactDetermination when settings.closedLoop is true.
highlights.userFacingImpact: Aims to prevent concurrent loop executions by performing the check-and-mark inside a LoopGuard and changes background-task ownership so the loop's async scope begins/ends the background task. The prior expiration handler/Task was removed, so if iOS expires the background task before loop completion, the coroutine completes on a best-effort basis rather than via the former handler. These behavioral changes may affect how loops complete when the app is backgrounded; a human review is recommended to assess runtime and safety impacts in production environments.

sources.title: APSManager thread safety fixes Part 2: LoopGuard actor + background-task lifecycle
Swap order in execute loop function to calculate Loop end date after determination
Add expiration handler in beginBackgroundTask method

items.summary: Bump APP_DEV_VERSION in Config.xcconfig from 0.8.3.3 to 0.8.3.4 (build/config metadata change).
items.userFacingImpact: Likely none for runtime or behavior; this is a development/build version metadata bump. If downstream workflows depend on APP_DEV_VERSION semantics, verify those separately.

includedPullRequests.title: APSManager thread safety fixes (Part 2)
includedPullRequests.body:
Closes the loop-serialization race and reworks the background-task
lifecycle around the loop's async scope. Sequel to Part 1, split out
of the original https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1145 to discuss LoopGuard + background-task handling separately from the bolus-progress actor.

**LoopGuard**

  The old check-then-act pattern read `isLooping.value`, returned from
  `canStartNewLoop()`, then later (after a suspension point) called
  `isLooping.send(true)`. Two concurrent `recommendsLoop` events could
  both observe `false` before either had sent `true`, so loop() could
  run twice. Replaced by a private `LoopGuard` actor that performs the
  check-and-mark-running step as a single atomic operation. isLooping
  becomes a downstream notification mechanism.

**Background-task lifecycle**

Removed the Task in the expiration handler which didn't provide a guarantee to be executed. Aligned with the example in https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/extending-your-app-s-background-execution-time. Side note: we probably need to refactor this when moving to Swift 6 as the expiration handler closure is Sendable and captures and mutates the var backgroundTaskID which isn't allowed when strict concurrency checking is on.

**Loop lifecycle consolidation**

  `canStartNewLoop`, `setupLoop`, `loopCompleted` are folded into
  loop() and a new single-exit-point `finalizeLoop`. `executeLoop` no
  longer calls finalize itself; the caller owns lifecycle. Open and
  closed loop paths in executeLoop now share the stats-recording
  block, with `enactDetermination` gated by `settings.closedLoop` at
  the end.

  `calculateLoopInterval` now takes `loopStartDate` as a parameter
  instead of reading the instance `lastLoopStartDate` — keeps the
  helper independent of the surrounding mutation order in loop().

includedPullRequests.title: APSManager thread safety fixes (Part 2)
includedPullRequests.body:
Closes the loop-serialization race and reworks the background-task
lifecycle around the loop's async scope. Sequel to Part 1, split out
of the original https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1145 to discuss LoopGuard + background-task handling separately from the bolus-progress actor.

**LoopGuard**

  The old check-then-act pattern read `isLooping.value`, returned from
  `canStartNewLoop()`, then later (after a suspension point) called
  `isLooping.send(true)`. Two concurrent `recommendsLoop` events could
  both observe `false` before either had sent `true`, so loop() could
  run twice. Replaced by a private `LoopGuard` actor that performs the
  check-and-mark-running step as a single atomic operation. isLooping
  becomes a downstream notification mechanism.

**Background-task lifecycle**

Removed the Task in the expiration handler which didn't provide a guarantee to be executed. Aligned with the example in https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/extending-your-app-s-background-execution-time. Side note: we probably need to refactor this when moving to Swift 6 as the expiration handler closure is Sendable and captures and mutates the var backgroundTaskID which isn't allowed when strict concurrency checking is on.

**Loop lifecycle consolidation**

  `canStartNewLoop`, `setupLoop`, `loopCompleted` are folded into
  loop() and a new single-exit-point `finalizeLoop`. `executeLoop` no
  longer calls finalize itself; the caller owns lifecycle. Open and
  closed loop paths in executeLoop now share the stats-recording
  block, with `enactDetermination` gated by `settings.closedLoop` at
  the end.

  `calculateLoopInterval` now takes `loopStartDate` as a parameter
  instead of reading the instance `lastLoopStartDate` — keeps the
  helper independent of the surrounding mutation order in loop().
